title: Add Tempest API test pipeline with GHCR image publishing for Keystone
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'Analogous to the execution of the unit tests for the Se'

Implement end-to-end Tempest API testing infrastructure: (1) `images/tempest/Dockerfile` — 3-stage build (python-base → venv-builder → tempest) installing Tempest 45.0.0 + keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0 + python-subunit + junitxml via uv pip with upper-constraints, OCI LABELs, running as openstack user (UID 42424); (2) `releases/2025.2/test-refs.yaml` — version pins for PyPI test tooling separate from git-ref-based source-refs.yaml; (3) `tests/tempest/keystone/tempest.conf` + include-tests.txt + exclude-tests.txt — per-service config targeting keystone-basic-api:5000/v3 with admin credentials from keystone-admin ExternalSecret, scoped to tempest.api.identity.* and keystone_tempest_plugin.tests.*; (4) `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` modification — append Tempest steps to e2e-keystone job after Chainsaw (resolve version from test-refs.yaml via yq, build/load image into kind, run tempest with --subunit, convert to JUnit XML, upload artifact e2e-tempest-keystone-junit-report with 14-day retention); (5) `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` modification — new build-tempest job with SBOM (anchore/sbom-action), Grype scanning, GitHub attestations, cosign signing, pushing to ghcr.io/c5c3/tempest:45.0.0-<branch>-<sha>; (6) `tests/container-images/verify_tempest.sh` — shell test with PASS/FAIL counters and exit-code guards verifying tempest --version, plugin presence, subunit2junitxml availability; (7) `Makefile` targets tempest-test / tempest-test SERVICE=keystone delegating to `hack/run-tempest.sh`; (8) `hack/run-tempest.sh` — orchestration script with set -euo pipefail, log() delimiters, configurable timeouts for local kind execution.

**Rationale:** Current CI validates operator Go code (unit/integration/E2E) and container image build correctness (verify_*.sh), but never exercises the upstream OpenStack code base packaged inside service images through real API calls. A packaging regression (wrong dependency version, missing config, broken WSGI pipeline) would pass all existing checks but fail in production. Tempest API tests close this gap by validating that the Keystone identity API responds correctly to standard OpenStack operations, catching functional regressions at the container image level before merge. Publishing the Tempest image to GHCR enables reuse across future services and local developer testing without rebuilding.

**Affected Areas:**
- images/tempest/Dockerfile
- releases/2025.2/test-refs.yaml
- tests/tempest/keystone/tempest.conf
- tests/tempest/keystone/include-tests.txt
- tests/tempest/keystone/exclude-tests.txt
- tests/container-images/verify_tempest.sh
- .github/workflows/ci.yaml
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- hack/run-tempest.sh
- Makefile
elaboration:
**Size:** 🏗️ large **Category:** infrastructure **Priority:** high ## Summary Add per-service Tempest API test execution to the CI pipeline and publish the Tempest image to GHCR. For Keystone, Tempest 45.0.0 (with keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0) runs targeted API tests (`tempest.api.identity.*` + `keystone_tempest_plugin.tests.*`) against the deployed Keystone service in a kind cluster, validating that the upstream code base packaged in the container image is functionally correct. The Tempest image follows the existing 3-stage build pattern (`python-base` → `venv-builder` → `tempest`) and is both built on-the-fly in CI (for E2E testing) and published to GHCR via `build-images.yaml` (for reuse by future consumers). This complements existing Go operator tests (unit + integration + E2E via Chainsaw) with upstream OpenStack API-level validation. **Note:** Architecture docs (`architecture/docs/08-container-images/index.md:105-118`) reference Tempest 41.0.0. This elaboration uses **45.0.0** per user specification — `releases/2025.2/test-refs.yaml` will pin this version as the source of truth. ## Scope **Included:** - Tempest container image build definition (`images/tempest/Dockerfile`) following the 3-stage pattern: `python-base` → `venv-builder` → `tempest` runtime. Unlike the Keystone Dockerfile (`images/keystone/Dockerfile`), no WSGI entry-point script or `--build-context =` mount is needed — Tempest installs from PyPI, not from cloned source. - Tempest 45.0.0 and keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0 installed via `uv pip install` with upper-constraints (`releases/2025.2/upper-constraints.txt`), plus `python-subunit` and `junitxml` for JUnit report conversion. - Version pinning in a new file `releases/2025.2/test-refs.yaml` (entries: `tempest: "45.0.0"`, `keystone-tempest-plugin: "0.19.0"`), separating PyPI test-tooling versions from the git-ref-based `source-refs.yaml`. - Per-service Tempest configuration (`tests/tempest/keystone/tempest.conf`) — `[identity]` auth URL pointing to the Keystone service deployed by the Chainsaw E2E (e.g., `http://keystone-basic-api.openstack.svc:5000/v3`), admin credentials from the existing `keystone-admin` ExternalSecret (provisioned by OpenBao bootstrap in `hack/deploy-infra.sh:577-603`), `[service_available]` with only `identity = true`. - Per-service test include/exclude lists (`tests/tempest/keystone/include-tests.txt` / `exclude-tests.txt`) — regex patterns controlling which Tempest tests run, analogous to `releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt` used for stestr unit tests. - CI workflow integration in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — new steps appended to the existing `e2e-keystone` job (`ci.yaml:205-334`) after Chainsaw test execution (`ci.yaml:292-295`): builds Tempest image, loads into kind, runs targeted tests against the deployed Keystone service, uploads JUnit XML to `_output/reports/`. - GHCR publishing in `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` — a dedicated `build-tempest` job (separate from the service build matrix) that builds the 3-stage image chain, tags with version metadata, pushes to `ghcr.io/c5c3/tempest:45.0.0--`, with SBOM generation (anchore/sbom-action), Grype vulnerability scanning, GitHub attestations, and cosign signing — consistent with the existing base/service image pattern. - `make tempest-test` / `make tempest-test SERVICE=keystone` Makefile targets (consistent with existing `make e2e` pattern at `Makefile:214-216`), delegating to a script in `hack/`. - JUnit XML report output to `_output/reports/` (via `--subunit` piped to `subunit2junitxml`), uploaded as CI artifact with 14-day retention (matching existing `e2e-${{ matrix.operator }}-junit-report` pattern at `ci.yaml:328-334`). **Excluded:** - Tempest Kubernetes Operator/CRD (`tempest.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1` documented in `architecture/docs/03-components/01-control-plane/04-optional-services.md:122-235` — separate Phase 9+ feature, YAGNI for CI). - Cross-service scenario tests (`tempest.scenario.*` — contradicts "test services individually", requires multi-service deployment with Nova, Neutron, Glance, Cinder). - Services beyond Keystone (no other service images or operators exist yet in the codebase). - Custom Tempest test authoring (uses upstream Tempest + official plugins only). - Production regression testing via CronJob scheduling (operator scope, not CI scope). - Modifications to `architecture/` directory (read-only reference). ## Visualization ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph CI["GitHub Actions CI Pipeline"] direction TB UT["Unit Tests\n#40;go test#41;"] IT["Integration Tests\n#40;envtest#41;"] E2E["E2E Chainsaw Tests\n#40;kind cluster#41;"] TT["Tempest API Tests\n#40;NEW - appended to e2e-keystone#41;"] end subgraph Build["Image Build Chain"] PB["python-base\n#40;ubuntu:noble + Python 3.12#41;"] VB["venv-builder\n#40;uv + build deps#41;"] SVC["keystone:2025.2\n#40;git clone + source mount#41;"] TI["tempest:45.0.0\n+ keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0\n#40;PyPI install, NEW#41;"] PB --> VB VB --> SVC VB --> TI end subgraph Kind["Kind Cluster #40;forge-e2e#41;"] INFRA["Infrastructure Stack\n#40;MariaDB, Memcached,\nOpenBao, FluxCD, ESO#41;"] KS["Keystone Deployment\n#40;Operator + keystone-basic CR#41;"] TP["Tempest Pod\n#40;NEW#41;"] INFRA --> KS KS --> TP end subgraph Publish["GHCR Publishing #40;build-tempest job, NEW#41;"] GHCR["ghcr.io/c5c3/tempest:45.0.0"] end SVC --> E2E SVC --> TT TI --> TT TI --> GHCR TT --> Kind TP -->|"JUnit XML"| REP["_output/reports/"] ``` ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant CI as CI Workflow
#40;e2e-keystone job#41; participant Kind as Kind Cluster
#40;forge-e2e#41; participant KS as Keystone Service
#40;keystone-basic-api:5000#41; participant TP as Tempest Container Note over CI,KS: Existing steps #40;ci.yaml:217-295#41; CI->>Kind: Create kind cluster + deploy infrastructure CI->>Kind: Install CRDs + deploy Keystone operator CI->>Kind: Apply Keystone CR #40;Chainsaw deploys this#41; Kind->>KS: Keystone pods Ready #40;port 5000#41; CI->>CI: Run Chainsaw E2E tests Note over CI,TP: New Tempest steps #40;appended after line 295#41; CI->>CI: Build python-base + venv-builder + tempest image CI->>Kind: kind load docker-image tempest:45.0.0 CI->>Kind: Run Tempest pod with mounted tempest.conf Kind->>TP: Start Tempest container TP->>TP: tempest init + load tempest.conf TP->>KS: Run tempest.api.identity.* tests TP->>KS: Run keystone_tempest_plugin.tests.* KS-->>TP: API responses TP->>TP: Generate subunit output TP-->>CI: Convert to JUnit XML in _output/reports/ CI->>CI: Upload artifact #40;e2e-tempest-keystone-junit-report#41; ``` ## Key Components - **`images/tempest/Dockerfile`** (NEW): 3-stage build (`python-base` → `venv-builder` → `tempest`). Unlike service Dockerfiles (e.g., `images/keystone/Dockerfile`) which mount cloned source via `--build-context`, Tempest installs directly from PyPI: `uv pip install tempest==45.0.0 keystone-tempest-plugin==0.19.0 python-subunit junitxml --constraint /tmp/upper-constraints.txt`. Runtime stage copies virtualenv from `venv-builder` to `python-base`, runs as `openstack` user (UID 42424, defined in `images/python-base/Dockerfile:24-25`). Uses named build context `--build-context upper-constraints=releases/2025.2` for constraint mounting. - **`releases/2025.2/test-refs.yaml`** (NEW): Separate version-pinning file for test tooling — `tempest: "45.0.0"` and `keystone-tempest-plugin: "0.19.0"`. Keeps PyPI-based test tool versions distinct from git-ref-based `source-refs.yaml` (which holds `keystone: "28.0.0"`). CI reads this file via `yq` to resolve versions for the Dockerfile build args and `uv pip install`. - **`tests/tempest/keystone/tempest.conf`** (NEW): Minimal Tempest configuration — `[identity]` section with `uri_v3 = http://keystone-basic-api.openstack.svc:5000/v3`, `[auth]` admin credentials sourced from the `keystone-admin` ExternalSecret (provisioned by OpenBao bootstrap at `hack/deploy-infra.sh:577-603`), `[service_available]` with only `identity = true`. - **`tests/tempest/keystone/include-tests.txt` / `exclude-tests.txt`** (NEW): Regex patterns controlling test scope. Include: `tempest.api.identity` and `keystone_tempest_plugin.tests`. Exclude: tests requiring services not deployed (compute, network, volume, image). Follows the pattern established by `releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt`. - **`.github/workflows/ci.yaml`** (MODIFIED): New steps appended to `e2e-keystone` job (after line 295) — resolve Tempest version from `test-refs.yaml`, build Tempest image chain (reusing `python-base` and `venv-builder` already built at lines 248-253), load into kind, run Tempest with `--subunit` output, convert to JUnit XML, upload as artifact `e2e-tempest-keystone-junit-report` with 14-day retention (matching existing pattern at lines 328-334). - **`.github/workflows/build-images.yaml`** (MODIFIED): New dedicated `build-tempest` job — builds the `python-base` → `venv-builder` → `tempest` chain without git-clone or source-mount steps. Reuses `python-base` and `venv-builder` images by digest (matching the existing `build-contexts: python-base=docker-image://...@${{ digest }}` pattern). Tags with version metadata, pushes to `ghcr.io/c5c3/tempest`, with SBOM generation (anchore/sbom-action), Grype scanning, GitHub attestations, and cosign signing — consistent with existing service images. - **`Makefile`** (MODIFIED): New targets `tempest-test` and `tempest-test SERVICE=keystone` for local execution (consistent with `e2e` target at line 214), delegating to `hack/run-tempest.sh`. - **`hack/run-tempest.sh`** (NEW): Script to build the Tempest image and run the container against the local kind cluster, handling image build, kind load, pod execution, and JUnit XML extraction. - **`_output/reports/`**: JUnit XML output from Tempest (via `subunit2junitxml`), alongside Chainsaw reports (configured in `tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml:38-42`).
